speaker: host
text: You keep returning to the factory town when you explain resilience.

speaker: guest
text: That town taught me systems fail long before leaders admit it.

speaker: host
text: So fragility is still your central metaphor.

speaker: guest
text: Yes because people trust brittle systems longer than they should.

speaker: host
text: And the mill closure remains your reference point.

speaker: guest
text: It is the cleanest proof I have that abstraction can hurt people.

speaker: host
text: You are still talking about fragility more than about product choices.

speaker: guest
text: Because product choices are just moral choices with diagrams.

speaker: host
text: That sounds close to what you said five minutes ago.

speaker: guest
text: Probably because the lesson really did not change.

speaker: host
text: Then give me the non-metaphor version of that lesson.

speaker: guest
text: Tell operators the truth before the dashboard looks clean.

speaker: host
text: So we are still on the same trust argument.

speaker: guest
text: Yes the trust argument and the factory story are the same thing.

speaker: host
text: It feels saturated now, not unresolved.

speaker: guest
text: That is fair, I am repeating the same scar from different angles.
